element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player starts with standard bullets; upgraded variants available through shops
Multiple bases per stage; destroying all triggers boss encounter
Defeated enemies and bases drop money; used for shop purchases
Touch marked balloons to access upgrades; prices escalate with each purchase
Various weapon types available; some have time limits, only one equipped at a time
Alternative attack type; limited quantity or time-based; equipped separately from weapons
Increases movement speed; permanent upgrade; affects mobility in combat
Opa-Opa can sprout feet and walk on ground; optional mobility option
Purchasable through shop system; loss of all upgrades upon death creates consequence
Required boss defeat to progress; appears after all bases destroyed
Succession of all previous bosses followed by final boss; conceptually popularized by this game
Encountered after boss rush sequence; game ending condition

typical_relief_triggers: Stages loop with player control of horizontal movement direction
Defeated bases yield money for progression
typical_tension_triggers: Loss of all upgrades upon death
Final boss rush with multiple sequential encounters
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical progression with resource management tension; player controls pace through horizontal exploration within looping stages; escalating difficulty toward boss encounters; final stage compresses tension through boss rush mechanic

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Shop balloons marked in environment; radar in arcade version indicates base locations

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stages 1-3 establish shop system, basic combat, and enemy patterns with manageable base counts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 4-6 increase enemy density and base count; upgrade paths become more strategic as costs escalate
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 7-8 demand mastery of weapon selection and engine upgrades; Stage 9 (boss rush) condenses prior challenges into rapid succession
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (Stage 1 completion) - introduces mandatory skill check
Stage 9 final level - boss rush mechanic multiplies tension through sequence of 8+ consecutive boss fights

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages 1-8 follow consistent structure: destroy N bases scattered in horizontally scrolling level, then face stage boss. Player controls direction of horizontal movement; stage loops until all bases destroyed.

Stage 9 abandons base destruction entirely. Player faces sequential rematch against all previously encountered bosses in succession, culminating in final boss fight. Creates crescendo tension through cumulative challenge.
Shop balloons appear throughout stages, creating temporal decision points. Player must decide when to interrupt combat flow to upgrade, weighing time cost against combat capability gains.

Death resets all temporary upgrades (weapons, bombs) but not permanent engine upgrades. Creates asymmetric recovery where player can rebuild partial capability but not fully return to pre-death state.
